task.text: A retried refund.succeeded webhook can credit a customer twice if the first delivery commits the ledger entry but times out before the idempotency marker. Make handling exactly-once at the application boundary without changing the public API.
successCriteria: restart after marker timeout does not re-credit
public RefundSucceeded event shape is unchanged

review.summary: {"status":"failed","findings":[{"severity":"high","file":"src/transactions/transaction-boundary.ts:17","message":"The idempotency cache is treated as authoritative: has(eventId) can return early without consulting the durable ledger. A stale, corrupt, or prematurely written marker therefore permanently suppresses a legitimate refund, contradicting the stated contract that the ledger owns uniqueness and the marker is repairable. The three pinned tests pass and RefundSucceeded is unchanged, but they do not cover this transaction-consistency failure."}]}

limitations: Requested model was not independently confirmed by an effective-model or reroute event.
